skill.name: incident-responder
skill.description: Guides SRE incident response for production outages, impact assessment, incident command, observability investigation, recovery, and blameless post-incident review.
skill.summary: Run structured SRE incident response from triage through recovery and postmortem.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed the static findings as false positives in SKILL.md. The backtick pattern at line 28 is Markdown around a file name, and the line 3 and line 33 matches are prose about system reliability, not weak cryptography or reconnaissance. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, executable scripts, network activity, or destructive instructions were found.

content.value_statement: Production incidents need fast triage, clear ownership, and reliable communication. This skill gives teams structured SRE guidance for response, recovery, and post-incident learning.
actual_capabilities: Classifies incident severity using impact, scope, and response expectations.
Defines incident command roles for coordination, communication, and technical response.
Guides observability-based investigation across metrics, logs, traces, dependencies, and capacity.
Recommends stabilization steps such as rollback assessment, scaling, throttling, and feature flags.
Structures stakeholder updates, status page communication, and support team briefings.
Supports blameless postmortems with timelines, root cause analysis, action items, and follow-up.
limitations: Does not directly connect to monitoring, paging, ticketing, or deployment systems.
Cannot verify live production state without user-provided data and tool access.
Does not replace company-specific incident policies, compliance rules, or executive decisions.
Requires human responders to approve risky mitigations and production changes.

use_cases.title: Coordinate an Active Outage
use_cases.description: Create a severity assessment, assign response roles, and plan the first mitigation steps.

use_cases.title: Improve Incident Communication
use_cases.description: Draft internal updates, executive summaries, support notes, and status page messages during an incident.

use_cases.title: Run a Blameless Review
use_cases.description: Turn incident notes into a timeline, contributing factors, customer impact, and tracked action items.

prompt_templates.title: Triage an Incident
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me triage this production incident. Impact: [describe users affected]. Symptoms: [describe alerts or reports]. Recent changes: [list changes].

prompt_templates.title: Set Incident Command
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an incident command plan for a [severity] incident affecting [service]. Include roles, communication cadence, escalation path, and first actions.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate with Observability
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an investigation plan using metrics, logs, traces, dependencies, and recent deploy data for this incident: [incident details].

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Postmortem
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn these incident notes into a blameless postmortem with timeline, impact, root cause, contributing factors, lessons, and action items: [notes].

output_examples.input: A checkout service has rising errors after a deployment, and customers cannot complete purchases.
output_examples.output: The response starts with severity classification, incident commander assignment, rollback assessment, payment dependency checks, customer impact tracking, and 15-minute stakeholder updates.

output_examples.input: An API is slow in one region, with elevated latency and retry traffic.
output: Check regional traffic, load balancer health, upstream dependencies, and capacity headroom.
Reduce retry amplification, shift traffic if safe, and monitor latency, errors, and saturation.
Document actions, timestamps, decision owners, and validation signals.

output_examples.input: The team needs a post-incident review after a database connection pool exhaustion incident.
output_examples.output: The skill organizes a timeline, impact summary, contributing factors, detection gaps, mitigation review, and follow-up actions for monitoring and capacity safeguards.

best_practices: Provide current impact, alerts, recent changes, and affected services before asking for an incident plan.
Use the guidance with your internal runbooks, escalation policies, and production change controls.
Keep a timestamped timeline during the incident so recovery and review steps stay evidence-based.
anti_patterns: Do not apply suggested mitigations to production without an owner, rollback path, and validation signal.
Do not focus on root cause analysis before stabilizing user impact during an active outage.
Do not use the skill as a substitute for legal, compliance, or customer notification approval.

faq.question: Can this skill operate monitoring tools directly?
faq.answer: No. It provides response guidance and analysis structure, but it needs user-provided data or separate tool access.

faq.question: Is it only for SRE teams?
faq.answer: No. It is useful for on-call engineers, incident commanders, engineering managers, and support leads.

faq.question: Can it help during a live outage?
faq.answer: Yes. It can structure severity assessment, roles, mitigation options, communication, and recovery validation.

faq.question: Does it write postmortems?
faq.answer: Yes. It can organize notes into a blameless postmortem with timeline, impact, causes, and action items.

faq.question: Does it replace company incident policy?
faq.answer: No. Teams should follow internal escalation, approval, compliance, and customer communication requirements.

faq.question: What information should I provide first?
faq.answer: Provide affected services, customer impact, alerts, recent changes, current mitigations, and any known constraints.
